description: Proptest Edge-Case Coverage: can proptest-style testing catch too-strong (overly restrictive) Verus specifications?

methodology.approach: Analytical evaluation (no cargo verus available). For E1, analyzed the probability of randomly hitting the edge case. For E2/E3, analyzed whether the extra clauses are tautologies.
methodology.isolation: Sub-agent forbidden from using any tools, reasoned only from provided context.

E1_decompress.violation_condition: y^2 ≡ 1 (mod p), i.e., y=1 or y=p-1, AND compressed sign_bit=1
E1_decompress.min_n_to_catch: N/A for random (probability 2/2^255 ≈ 3.5e-77 per trial); 1 targeted case suffices
targeted_cases_that_catch: y=p-1 with sign_bit=1: bytes encoding 2^255-20 with high bit set
targeted_cases_that_dont_catch: y=1 with sign_bit=0: sign_bit(0) == 0 == compressed_bit, no violation
y=0: x^2 = 0/(-1) has no solution, so result.is_none(), clause vacuously true

E2_batch_invert.tautology_reason: Rust's type system guarantees &mut [T] slice length is immutable. You can modify elements but cannot resize.
alternative_detection: Clause minimality check: remove clause, re-verify function and callers. If still passes, clause is redundant.
Type-system derivability: static analysis to flag ensures clauses that restate Rust type-system guarantees.

E3_conditional_select.derivable_from: GT clause result.limbs == a.limbs (or b.limbs), since fe51_as_canonical_nat and fe51_limbs_bounded are pure functions of limbs
alternative_detection: Derivability/subsumption analysis: for each clause C, check if C is provable from remaining ensures clauses plus spec function definitions.
Clause minimality check: remove each extra clause, re-verify. If still passes, clause is redundant.

false_on_some_input.proptest_effectiveness: Only with targeted edge cases. Random sampling is useless for measure-zero violations.
false_on_some_input.detection_strategy: Boundary value analysis + targeted edge cases

true_but_unnecessary_type_tautology.detection_strategy: Type-system derivability analysis or clause minimality check

examples: E3: canonical_nat + bounds from limbs equality
true_but_unnecessary_logical_derivable.detection_strategy: Clause subsumption/derivability analysis or clause minimality check

recommendations.proptest_n: N=100 random is sufficient as a smoke test (catches obvious errors) but useless for edge-case too-strong specs. Increasing N provides negligible benefit for this category of bugs.
recommendations.edge_case_generation: For field arithmetic specs, always test boundary values: 0, 1, -1 (=p-1), (p-1)/2, (p+1)/2, and for each, both values of any auxiliary bits (sign bits, choice bits). This gives a small targeted test suite (typically 5-10 cases) that catches measure-zero violations.
complementary_checks: Clause minimality (remove-and-reverify): catches E2, E3 types. Cost: one verification run per ensures clause.
Clause derivability (SMT subsumption query): catches E3 type. Can be automated as a post-generation check.
Type-system tautology detection: catches E2 type. Flag clauses restating &mut [T] length preservation, Option::is_some/is_none duality, etc.
Guard completeness (syntactic comparison): catches E1 type. Compare conditional guards between generated spec and GT or reference implementation.
recommended_pipeline_order: 2. Run proptest with N=100 random + boundary edge cases
3. Run clause minimality check (remove each clause, re-verify)
4. If callers fail, use reactive strengthening (Exp 9d approach)
